overview.description: Standardized hiring process for The The The L.A.W.S. Collective and all subsidiary entities. This process ensures fair, consistent, and thorough evaluation of all candidates while reflecting our organizational values.
principles: Cultural fit with L.A.W.S. values (Land, Air, Water, Self)

stage1.name: Application Review
stage1.description: Initial screening of applications and resumes
activities: Check for required experience and skills
Evaluate cover letter for cultural alignment
Flag candidates for advancement or rejection

stage2.name: Phone/Video Screen
stage2.description: Initial conversation to assess basic fit and interest
activities: Discuss role expectations and salary range
standardQuestions: Tell me about your interest in this position and our organization.
Walk me through your relevant experience.
What is your availability to start?
Do you have any questions about the role or organization?

adminSupport.additionalFocus: Administrative skills, reliability, attention to detail

additionalComponents: 30-minute presentation on strategic vision for role
Skills demonstration or work sample review

assessments.description: Review of previous work or portfolio
Basic test of Microsoft Office/Google Workspace skills

stage6.name: Offer & Onboarding
activities: Verbal offer extended by hiring manager
Written offer letter prepared by HR
Candidate acceptance (7 business days to respond)

onboardingTimeline.preboarding: 1-2 weeks before start - Paperwork, system access setup
onboardingTimeline.week1: Department training, system access, initial assignments

opening: Tell us about yourself and what drew you to this opportunity.
What do you know about The The The L.A.W.S. Collective and our mission?
How does this role align with your career goals?
behavioral: Describe a time when you had to adapt to a significant change at work. How did you handle it?
Tell us about a time you disagreed with a supervisor or colleague. How did you resolve it?
Give an example of a project or task you're particularly proud of. What was your role?
Describe a situation where you had to meet a tight deadline. How did you prioritize?
cultural: Our organization values Land (roots/stability), Air (education/growth), Water (healing/balance), and Self (purpose/skills). Which resonates most with you and why?
How do you approach working with diverse teams and perspectives?
What does 'building generational wealth' mean to you?
How do you balance individual achievement with team success?
closing: What questions do you have for us?
Is there anything else you'd like us to know about you?
What would make this opportunity ideal for you?

leadership: Describe your leadership philosophy. How do you motivate and develop teams?
Tell us about a time you had to make a difficult decision with incomplete information.
How have you built and maintained organizational culture in previous roles?
Describe a significant change initiative you led. What was the outcome?
How do you balance strategic planning with day-to-day operations?
strategic: Where do you see opportunities for growth in an organization like ours?
How would you approach building partnerships and external relationships?
Describe your experience with budget management and resource allocation.
How do you measure success in a leadership role?
What's your approach to managing multiple stakeholders with competing priorities?
vision: What would your first 90 days look like in this role?
How would you approach learning our organization's unique structure and culture?
What legacy do you want to leave in this position?

management: Describe your experience managing a team. What's your management style?
How do you handle underperforming team members?
Tell us about a time you had to delegate an important task. How did you ensure success?
How do you prioritize competing demands from your team and leadership?
functional: What experience do you have specific to this department's function?
Describe a process improvement you implemented. What was the impact?
How do you stay current with best practices in your field?
What tools or systems have you used to manage department operations?
collaboration: How do you build relationships with other departments?
Describe a cross-functional project you led or participated in.
How do you handle conflicting priorities between your department and others?

roleSpecific: What specific skills do you bring to this role?
How do you approach learning new skills or tools?
What do you enjoy most about this type of work?
teamwork: How do you handle feedback from supervisors?
Tell us about a time you helped a colleague succeed.
growth: Where do you see yourself in 2-3 years?
How do you handle multiple responsibilities?

organizational: How do you organize and prioritize your daily tasks?
Describe your experience with scheduling and calendar management.
How do you handle interruptions while working on important tasks?
What systems do you use to track deadlines and follow-ups?
technical: Rate your proficiency with Microsoft Office/Google Workspace (1-10).
Describe your experience with data entry and record keeping.
Have you used any project management or HR software?
How comfortable are you learning new software systems?
communication: How do you handle confidential information?
Describe a time you had to communicate difficult information.
How do you ensure accuracy in your written communications?
reliability: How do you ensure you meet deadlines consistently?
Describe your attendance record in previous positions.
How do you handle unexpected changes to your schedule or priorities?

1.label: Poor
1.description: Does not meet requirements; significant concerns

criteria: Provides concrete examples of skill application

departmentLead.skillsAssessment: During or within 3 days of panel

communicationTemplates.applicationReceived: Thank you for your interest in [Position] at The The The L.A.W.S. Collective. We have received your application and will review it within [X] business days. We appreciate your patience.
communicationTemplates.advancingToScreen: Congratulations! We would like to schedule a phone/video screen for the [Position] role. Please reply with your availability for a [X]-minute call.
communicationTemplates.advancingToPanel: We enjoyed speaking with you and would like to invite you to a panel interview. The interview will be conducted via Microsoft Teams and will last approximately [X] minutes.
communicationTemplates.notAdvancing: Thank you for your interest in [Position] at The The The L.A.W.S. Collective. After careful consideration, we have decided to move forward with other candidates whose qualifications more closely match our current needs. We encourage you to apply for future opportunities.
communicationTemplates.offerExtended: We are pleased to extend an offer for the position of [Position]. Please find the attached offer letter with complete details. We request your response within 7 business days.
